The Agentic Commerce Platform
KYA identity and agent payments
Explore Use Cases
Launch Skyfire ->
WHAT IS SKYFIRE?
Payments and Identity Built for the AI Economy
Skyfire empowers AI to process payments, verify identities and access essential services without human intervention.  From API account creation to monetizing websites, we drive commerce for the world's fastest-growing consumer base: AI agents.
Learn how it works
BENEFITS AT A GLANCE
Why Skyfire?
The trusted network for global payments that move at the speed of AI.  Drive the future of commerce with instant payments and Know Your Agent (KYA) identity.
ADVANTAGE 1
Autonomous Payments
Pay instantly without human involvement.
ADVANTAGE 2
New Revenue Streams
Sell to AI and open new, untapped customers and markets.
ADVANTAGE 3
KYA: Identity Verification
Give your agents a verified identity for autonomous account creation and access.
Learn how it works
THE PROCESS
Seamless  AI Transactions in Action
Skyfire empowers AI agents to conduct transactions effortlessly. They can pay businesses, verify their identity, and access essential data and services in real-time.
Service Request
AI agent makes a service request via API
KYA: Identity Verification
Service verifies agent identity and payment
Payment Processing
Service delivered and agent is charged
Featured in
Ready to Power AI Transactions?
Empower AI to pay, verify, and transact autonomously with seamless access to services
Explore Use Cases
Launch Skyfire ->

Developer Documentation
Identity and Payments for AI. This guide provides technical instructions for using the Skyfire platform as both a Buyer and Seller.
Overview
Skyfire enables programmatic interaction between
Buyers
and
Sellers
using a token-based identity and payment protocol.
Each user manages one or more agent accounts (Buyer or Seller).  Buyers interact with seller websites, APIs, MCP servers, and services by presenting Skyfire tokens:
kya
(identity)
pay
(payment)
kya-pay
(combined identity and payment)
Platform Structure
Users
A
User
is the top-level entity on the Skyfire platform. Users typically represent an individual person or business.
Users:
Manage one or more
agent accounts
Maintain a verified identity
Control API keys and wallet access
A user's verified identity may be leveraged by their agents for:
Account creation
Authentication
Access to seller services
Agent Accounts
Agent accounts are the operational entities that interact with Skyfire APIs.
Each agent:
Is tied to exactly one user
Has exactly one role:
Buyer
or
Seller
Is accessed programmatically via unique API keys.
Each user may have:
One
Buyer agent account
(created by default at signup).
One optional
Seller agent account
(created via the Skyfire Dashboard)
Each agent may have one or more API keys for secure access.
Buyer Agent
Acts on behalf of the user to discover and purchase services or tools from sellers.
Capabilities:
Can create accounts with seller services
Subscribe to or purchase services
Generate:
kya
tokens (identity)
pay
tokens (payment)
kya-pay
tokens (identity + payment)
Each buyer agent has a
wallet
, funded by the user, used to transact with sellers.
When calling a seller service, the buyer includes the appropriate token as specified by the seller (e.g. HTTP header or request body).
Seller Agent
Enables a user to publish and monetize services, tools, or APIs.
Seller agents:
Must be explicitly created via the Skyfire Dashboard
Register
Seller Services
Define identity and payment requirements for buyer requests
Verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries to:
Authenticate the buyer (
kya
)
Charge the buyer (
pay
or
kya-pay
)
Sellers receive payments via Skyfire's settlement system once services are delivered.
Wallets
Each agent account comes with an associated wallet, managed by Skyfire.
Buyers
fund their wallets to purchase services and tools
Sellers
receive funds in their wallets after successfully charging buyer tokens and delivering the service
Skyfire handles
settlement and transfer
of funds between wallets after a successful transaction.
Seller Services
Seller services are the products, tools, or APIs made available by seller agents to buyers.
A seller can define
multiple seller services
under one seller agent.
Services may include:
Remote/local MCP servers
APIs
Websites
Each Seller Service specifies:
Pricing
Identity requirements
Accepted token types
Token format and expected delivery method (e.g.
skyfire-pay-id
header)
Identity Requirements
Seller Services may optionally enforce identity requirements.
If
no identity requirements
are specified:
Any buyer can create tokens for it
The buyer principal's email address will be included in the
hid
claim
If identity requirements are specified:
Only buyers that satisfy those identity requirements can create tokens for it
Rules
:
If only
business identity
is required:
Only buyers that have verified their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified individual identity CANNOT create tokens
If only
individual identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified their individual identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified business identity CANNOT create tokens
If both
individual and business identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified either their individual identity or their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity at all CANNOT create tokens
Buyers with no verified identity cannot create tokens for restricted services
Services can be:
Paid
, require
pay
or
kya-pay
tokens
Free
, require
kya
tokens (identity only)
Updated
about 2 months ago
What’s Next
Understanding KYA+Pay Tokens
KYA+Pay Tokens

analysis.name: Skyfire
analysis.value_proposition: Skyfire gives AI agents a verified identity and payment wallet so they can autonomously transact with APIs, websites, and services without human involvement.
analysis.ideal_customer: Developers and businesses building or deploying AI agents that need to autonomously purchase services, and API/SaaS vendors who want to monetize access to AI agents as customers.

features.name: autonomous payments
features.description: AI agents can initiate and complete payments without any human intervention.

features.name: kya identity verification
features.description: Know Your Agent (KYA) protocol that verifies an agent's identity before granting access or charging payment.

features.name: kya tokens
features.description: JWT-based identity tokens that assert a buyer agent's verified identity to seller services.

features.name: pay tokens
features.description: JWT-based payment tokens that authorize a seller to charge a buyer agent's wallet for a service.

features.name: kya-pay tokens
features.description: Combined identity and payment token that simultaneously authenticates the agent and authorizes a charge.

features.name: buyer agent accounts
features.description: Operational agent accounts that discover, subscribe to, and purchase seller services on behalf of a user.

features.name: seller agent accounts
features.description: Operational agent accounts that publish, configure, and monetize services or APIs to buyer agents.

features.name: agent wallet
features.description: Per-agent wallet funded by the user; buyers spend from it, sellers receive settlement into it.

features.name: wallet funding
features.description: Users top up buyer agent wallets to fund autonomous purchases.

features.name: settlement system
features.description: Skyfire-managed transfer of funds between buyer and seller wallets after successful service delivery.

features.name: api keys
features.description: Per-agent API keys for programmatic, secure access to the Skyfire platform.

features.name: seller services registry
features.description: Sellers define and publish one or more named services under a seller agent, each with pricing and identity requirements.

features.name: service discovery
features.description: Skyfire Directory that lets buyer agents find available seller services.

features.name: verified service guarantee
features.description: Platform-level assurance that listed seller services are verified.

features.name: mcp server support
features.description: Seller services can expose remote or local MCP servers as purchasable resources.

features.name: automated account creation
features.description: Buyer agents can autonomously create accounts with seller services using their verified KYA identity.

features.name: individual identity verification
features.description: Sellers can restrict services to buyers who have verified their individual (personal) identity.

features.name: business identity verification
features.description: Sellers can restrict services to buyers who have verified their business identity.

features.name: identity requirements enforcement
features.description: Per-service policy engine that gates token issuance based on buyer's verified identity level (none, individual, business, or either).

features.name: jwks token verification
features.description: Sellers verify incoming KYA/PAY tokens using standard JWKS libraries, enabling framework-agnostic integration.

features.name: token delivery configuration
features.description: Sellers specify how tokens should be delivered per service (e.g., HTTP header skyfire-pay-id or request body).

features.name: per-service pricing configuration
features.description: Each seller service independently defines its own pricing for buyer agents.

features.name: multi-agent account management
features.description: A single user can manage one buyer and one seller agent account, each with multiple API keys.

features.name: skyfire dashboard
features.description: Web UI for creating seller agent accounts, managing services, and viewing wallet state.

features.name: buyer onboarding workflow
features.description: Guided setup flow for buyer account creation, identity verification, and wallet funding.

features.name: seller onboarding workflow
features.description: Guided setup flow for seller account creation, service registration, and token requirement configuration.

features.name: api-based service access
features.description: Buyer agents call seller APIs and include tokens in the request to authenticate and pay in a single call.

features.name: free service support
features.description: Seller services can be configured as free (identity-only, using kya tokens) with no payment required.

features.name: new revenue streams for sellers
features.description: Enables existing API/web businesses to monetize access specifically to AI agent customers.

differentiators: Know Your Agent (KYA) — purpose-built identity verification protocol for AI agents, analogous to KYC for humans
Payments move at the speed of AI — no human approval step in the transaction loop
First mover framing as 'The Agentic Commerce Platform' targeting AI agents as a consumer base
Combined identity + payment in a single token (kya-pay), reducing round-trips
Enables sellers to gate access by verified identity level (individual vs. business) for AI buyers
